meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Freelancers: Scale Beyond Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building blind. Use this advanced analytics checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-converting, data-driven SaaS apps for clients.
content.answer_block: To escape the 'basic app' trap, implement event-driven telemetry that maps user intent to business outcomes. By integrating Antigravity for automated schema generation and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform static interfaces into high-conversion engines, providing your clients with the actionable data they actually crave.
content.intro:
You’ve shipped the MVP, the client is happy, but the product is a ghost town. You’re stuck in the cycle of building features without proof, watching apps stall because you lack the visibility to pivot. Every line of code you write without a data feedback loop is a gamble on your professional reputation.

It’s time to move from 'coder' to 'product engineer.' By embedding sophisticated analytics into your workflow, you stop guessing and start optimizing. This isn't just about tracking clicks; it's about engineering a system that tells you exactly why a user stays, why they churn, and how to scale their revenue.

steps.title: Define the Telemetry Schema
steps.description: Identify the core conversion events that define your client's success. Don't track everything; track the metrics that move the needle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and generate a JSON schema for a robust analytics event system. Focus on tracking user onboarding steps, feature engagement, and conversion drop-offs. Ensure the schema supports user ID, event name, timestamp, and metadata properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the analytics event schema and create a base trackEvent utility function.'

steps.title: Automate Event Injection
steps.description: Use AI to inject event tracking across your component library without manual boilerplate. This ensures consistent data collection across the entire UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current React component library to inject analytics tracking on button clicks and form submissions automatically using a Higher-Order Component or a custom hook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a custom useAnalytics hook and apply it to all interactive elements in /src/components.'

steps.title: Implement Client-Side Middleware
steps.description: Centralize your analytics logic to handle data sanitization, batching, and error handling before dispatching to your backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust event-processing middleware that batches analytics events to reduce network overhead and handles offline retries for mobile-first users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a middleware for our event dispatcher that adds session context and batches events every 5 seconds.'

steps.title: Setup Automated Reporting Triggers
steps.description: Don't wait for a manual report. Configure your system to push critical insights directly to Slack or a dashboard when conversion KPIs shift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated workflow script that evaluates user conversion rates and triggers an alert if the drop-off rate exceeds 20% compared to the 7-day moving average.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement a cron job or webhook listener that monitors analytics data and sends a Slack notification on KPI deviation.'

steps.title: Validate and Debug Data Streams
steps.description: Use automated testing to ensure your events are firing correctly across different environments, preventing 'data rot' in your production database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests that simulate user flows and verify that the correct analytics events are dispatched to the tracking endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /test 'Create e2e tests for user signup flow ensuring every step triggers the expected analytics event.'

pro_tips: Always namespace your events (e.g., 'user:onboarding:started') to keep your analytics warehouse clean and queryable.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your analytics payloads; hash your user IDs for privacy compliance.
Implement 'virtual page views' for SPA transitions to ensure your funnel tracking doesn't break on client-side routing.
Use sampling for high-volume events to keep your data costs low without sacrificing statistical significance.
Always include the app version in every event metadata block to track how feature changes impact performance over time.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, spend 30 minutes in your analytics dashboard to identify the top 3 'friction points' where users currently drop off, and prioritize your next sprint around fixing those specific bottlenecks.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build data-driven apps that clients pay a premium for. Stop guessing and start shipping with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
